question: You're Pedri. Betis's #6 is pressing hard. What do you do?

options.text: Play it back to the center back
Turn and play into Rashford in the center circle
Shield the ball and wait for support
Play a one-time pass through the lines

explanation: Pedri's shoulder check before receiving tells him everything. Yamal is already making the run, and the #6's press shifts toward Rashford, closing that lane while opening the channel behind the line. The one-time pass finds Yamal before the defense can recover. Option A gives Betis time to reset, option B runs directly into the pressing lane, and option C lets the space disappear.

question: You're Pedri. A defender is closing fast with the play slowing down. What do you do?

options.text: Play it wide left to Rashford on the wing
Play the ball back to player who just passed to him
Cut back against the defender's momentum and play it to the right winger
Carry the ball forward and drive into the space ahead

explanation: Rashford wide left is still getting set and a defender is already eyeing that switch. The obvious ball is left, and the defense is leaning that way too. Pedri cuts back against that momentum instead, finds the right winger in space, and Yamal's run away from the play opens the lane. Option A is available but easy to read, option B is back into the pressure, and option D can be forced.

question: You're Pedri. A defender is on your back and Betis is pressing high. What do you do?

options.text: Half turn and try to play forward through the pressure
Take a touch and play it back to the keeper
Play into Cubarsi at right center back
Half turn to play into the left back

explanation: Every forward and wide option has a Betis player close by. The teammate to the left is marked, and turning into the defender on his back with no support ahead is a losing battle. Taking a touch to control and playing back is the safest way to keep possession and let Barcelona reset. Option A risks playing straight into pressure, option C looks available but the right center back is tracked, and option D the left back is being closely marked

question: You're Pedri receiving in midfield with pressure from the right. What do you do?

options.text: Play it back to where it came from — the center back
Lay it wide right to the right back on the touchline
Half turn into the space and play the ball forward
Pass to the left center back to switch the build-up

explanation: The Betis #8 is positioned to cut off the left center back, so that switch is risky. The right back is already under pressure, so going wide right just moves the problem to a crowded side. Playing back to the center back is safe but low-value. The half turn lets Pedri face forward and attack the open channel before Betis can reorganize.

question: You're Pedri. You receive from Yamal with a defender on your back. What do you do?

options.text: Take a touch and half turn to play out
Play it first time back to Balde at left back
Shoot first time at goal
One-touch through ball into Rashford's run

explanation: The defender is already closing from behind, so taking a touch invites contact before Pedri can do anything useful. Playing back to the left back is safe but kills the chance because Rashford's run is live right now. Shooting is tempting, but the angle is tight and the pressure is immediate. The one-touch through ball is the only option that uses the defender's momentum against him and finds Rashford before the line recovers.
